concept: Interface / Defined Place or Means of System Interaction
description: The defined means or place where two things interact, sharper than a bare boundary and wider than any one screen or plug. Naming an interface is naming exactly where and how the interaction happens.
sound_symbolism: Firm 'no' establishes a defined edge, while the complete relational form 'wiso' moves across it in reciprocal connection. The word holds separation and contact together without erasing either or promising that exchange will succeed.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha nowiso lo terura lorea.' (This interface connects the systems.) State what crosses the interface with ordinary transfer, input, output, or signal clauses. Distinct from 'norui' (boundary), 'lorea' (connect), and a visual control surface alone.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The concept attends to the shaped meeting between distinct forms, where connection depends on how edges are made to relate.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: An interface names the conditions of interaction without assuming that either side understands, accepts, or can use what the other presents.

tags.creation: a designed means through which systems or components interact
tags.dialogue: an informational interface can mediate exchange without guaranteeing understanding
tags.spatial: a boundary or contact location across which exchange occurs
